includes: project config discovery and CLI policy controls
directory and glob analysis with aggregate batch reporting
experimental opt-in LLM prompt review with provider configuration
docs, examples, CI validation, and an automated Changesets-based release workflow
baseline.summary: This is a real MVP. It is useful today, but it is still missing richer LLM guidance, rewrite suggestions, browser workflows, editor integrations, suppression mechanics, and team collaboration.

releasePlan.deliverables: Deterministic linting foundation, profiles, core library, CLI, browser analyzer, docs foundation, and release-ready validation workflows.
Project config discovery, CLI policy controls, and the first CI-friendly defaults for teams adopting PromptScore in repositories.
Directory and glob analysis with aggregate batch reporting, shared release version syncing, and stronger changelog/release automation.
Optional LLM-backed prompt review behind explicit opt-in, provider configuration, issue-specific guidance, benchmark fixtures, and regression tracking for false positives.
Hosted accounts, private workspaces, report history, shared policies, team review flows, cloud sync for reports, and early billing foundations.
Stable config format, stable JSON schema, stable core API, official editor integration, production-ready GitHub Action, hosted workspaces GA, and the first enterprise-ready governance features.

releaseMeaning.summary: made PromptScore configurable in real projects.
makes PromptScore usable across repeat repository workflows.
made PromptScore smarter without losing its deterministic identity.
should prove that a hosted collaboration layer adds real value.
should signal stability, integrations, and long-term compatibility.

immediateNextMoves: Keep docs, site copy, and changelogs aligned with the shipped version.
Continue strengthening the release checklist, changelog discipline, and browser/CLI parity.
Expand the `v0.4.x` line around reference-backed explanations, rewrite suggestions, and stronger LLM regression fixtures before expanding into hosted features.
Keep commercial details private until the hosted `v0.5` scope is real enough to announce confidently.
